blurb: The parable of the Great Supper tells of a host whose table is ready yet whose invited guests plead off with fields, oxen, and new brides. When the expected refuse, the master sends servants to drag in the blind and lame until every seat is full. The warning is gentle but firm: the kingdom is not withheld from us; we withhold ourselves from it by clutching lesser loves. Today let the tug of lesser tables grow strangely dim so that the laughter of the eternal feast may reach your ears.

verses.text: And when one of them that reclined with him heard these things, he said unto him, Blessed is he that shall eat bread in the kingdom of God.
But he said unto him, A certain man made a great supper, and bade many:
And sent his servant at supper time to say to them that were bidden, Come; for all things are now ready.
And they all with one consent began to make excuse. The first said unto him, I have bought a piece of ground, and I must needs go and see it: I pray thee have me excused.
